_doc: Knobs for the Ocean Drive vertical slice. All distances in cm; everything snaps to GRID. Edit these, re-run generate_slice.py. See README.md.
_geo: Barrier-island slice on TRUE Caliber City coordinates so it is literally the future city's Ocean Drive. Bay is west of x=bay_edge, ocean east of x=ocean_edge; land between.
_foundation: World base for the new map. 'cesium' => georeferenced real Miami terrain + CesiumSunSky (no flat plane, no basic sky rig); the stylized CityBeachStrip city sits on top. 'flat' => make_open_city's flat ground plane + sky rig. lat/lon = build_miami.py's South Beach origin. tileset 'world_terrain' avoids photoreal buildings clashing with our stylized ones; 'google_3d' streams the real city instead.
_roads: N-S roads run along Y (the island long axis); cross streets run along X every cross_spacing. centerline x for each N-S road + class. ROW half-widths come from road_classes.
_overlap: Constants from the spec overlap_rules. setback dilates ROWs into the no-build BLOCKMASK; plot_inset is the perimeter gap inside a block; side_rear_inset shrinks the building inside its plot.
_columns: Per gap between adjacent N-S roads (outer->ocean side). role drives building palette; party_wall=true => plots share Y walls (gap 0) on the signature Art Deco row; frontage = setback toward the fronting road (east).
_hero: Hero hotels (LI_Resort) anchor the strip ends of the hero_row column; mid-rise fills between. hero_end_blocks = how many blocks at each Y extreme are heroes.
_recenter: If true, the emitted placement_plan + graphs are shifted so the slice center sits at world origin (the Cesium georeference point = real South Beach). The solver keeps true spec coords internally (overlap is translation-invariant), so the slice still composes into the full city later. header.world_offset records the shift.
